goals: Expose `apply_lora(model, clip, path, strength_model, strength_clip) -> tuple[Any, Any]`
Return patched copies of model and CLIP — originals are never mutated.
Match the lazy-import and CPU-safe patterns of every prior pycomfy module.
Support LoRA stacking via simple call chaining (no extra API needed).
Be directly consumable by `it_07 (ImagePipeline)` with no adapter code.

userStories.title: Apply a single LoRA to a model+CLIP pair
userStories.description: As a Python developer (or a higher-level pycomfy module), I want to call `apply_lora(model, clip, "/path/to/lora.safetensors", 0.8, 0.8)`, so that I receive patched `(model, clip)` copies with the LoRA weights applied and the originals unchanged.

acceptanceCriteria.text: The function returns a `tuple[Any, Any]` of `(patched_model, patched_clip)`.
The original `model` and `clip` objects are not mutated (ComfyUI's internal `.clone()`
`apply_lora` is importable as `from pycomfy import apply_lora`
No `comfy.*` or `torch` import occurs at module level — all ComfyUI imports deferred
Typecheck / lint passes (`ruff check .` and `mypy pycomfy/` produce no new violations).

userStories.title: Stack multiple LoRAs via chaining
userStories.description: As a Python developer, I want to chain multiple `apply_lora` calls using the output of one as the input to the next, so that I can compose any number of LoRA weights without a special multi-LoRA API.

acceptanceCriteria.text: The following pattern produces a correctly double-patched model+CLIP:
Each intermediate result is independent — applying the second LoRA does not
No additional API surface is required; the test demonstrates the chaining pattern.

userStories.title: pytest coverage for apply_lora
userStories.description: As a developer maintaining pycomfy, I want pytest tests that validate `apply_lora` behavior without GPU or real checkpoints, so that regressions are caught in CPU-only CI.

acceptanceCriteria.text: The test imports `apply_lora` from `pycomfy` (public surface only, not internals).
A mock test verifies that `comfy.sd.load_lora_for_models` is called with the correct
A mock test verifies that `comfy.utils.load_torch_file` is called with the given
A mock test verifies that chaining two `apply_lora` calls results in
All existing tests continue to pass (`uv run pytest`).
Tests run on CPU without loading any real checkpoint or LoRA file.

functionalRequirements.description: ** `apply_lora(model: Any, clip: Any, path: str | os.PathLike, strength_model: float, strength_clip: float) -> tuple[Any, Any]` — public function in `pycomfy/lora.py`.
** The LoRA state dict is loaded via `comfy.utils.load_torch_file(path, safe_load=True)`,
** The patching is performed via `comfy.sd.load_lora_for_models(model, clip, lora, strength_model, strength_clip)`
